title: Regex Matching
description: Regular expression engines that support backreferences use backtracking NFA simulation. When a partial match fails (e.g., a quantifier consumed too many characters), the engine backtracks to the last choice point and tries an alternative path through the NFA state graph.

title: Constraint Solvers
description: Sudoku solvers and general constraint satisfaction problem (CSP) engines use backtracking search with constraint propagation. They assign values to variables one at a time, propagate constraints to reduce domains, and backtrack when a contradiction is detected — pruning large portions of the search space.

title: Compiler Register Allocation
description: Compilers model register allocation as a graph coloring problem where variables are nodes and edges connect simultaneously live variables. When greedy coloring fails (not enough registers), the compiler backtracks by spilling a variable to memory and retrying the coloring with the reduced interference graph.
